job_description:
About Us:

The Jonas Software Group of Companies acquires, manages, and builds industry-specific software companies globally. With over 100+ acquired companies and 2,500+ employees globally, we have long realized that our greatest asset is our employee base.

OnTap Data is a Melbourne-based technology company powering smarter decisions for the Australian liquor and retail industry. With over 25 years of experience, we build purpose-built software tools that help suppliers, retailers, and wholesalers aggregate, report on, and act on their sales data. Our clients include some of Australia’s largest liquor brands and retail groups. We’re a small, high-impact team where your work genuinely matters.



About the role: 

OnTap Data is seeking a curious, self‑driven Junior Software Engineer (Data/AI) to join our team and play a hands‑on role in building, improving, and modernising the technology that underpins our data products. This role involves working across our stack, getting stuck into real problems, and helping shape how we evolve our platforms. If you enjoy working with data, pick up new technologies with ease, and thrive in environments that empower you to work independently, we want to hear from you!



Key Responsibilities:

Search & Analytics Development
• Build and optimise Elasticsearch-based search and analytics features.
• Improve performance and reliability of reporting functionality.

Modernising Legacy Systems
• Migrate Microsoft Access–based systems into modern, scalable solutions.

Tech Stack Evolution
• Contribute to the ongoing modernisation of applications, infrastructure, and data pipelines.

Data Processing & Integration
• Clean, transform, and integrate large, messy datasets from multiple sources.
• Make real‑world data accurate, usable, and reliable.

End‑to‑End Development
• Design, build, test, and deploy features across the full stack.

Teamwork & Stakeholder Engagement
• Collaborate with a small development team.
• Work directly with internal stakeholders to understand requirements.


Skills, Experience and Qualifications Required:

1–2 years of professional software development experience.
 Genuine interest in data — whether querying databases, writing simple ETL processes, or building search functionality.
Interest in AI‑driven development practices and a willingness to use AI tools and workflows (e.g., BMAD) to build software more effectively.
Familiarity with relational databases and SQL; bonus if you’ve worked with Microsoft Access or legacy systems.
Willingness to learn Elasticsearch or experience with similar search/analytics technologies.
 Curious, self‑driven, and comfortable investigating problems, reading documentation, and learning independently.
Able to work autonomously and manage your own work without daily oversight.
 Strong attention to detail, analytical thinking, and clear communication skills.
The following are advantageous but not required:

 Exposure to languages such as Python, C#, .NET, JavaScript, PHP, Go, or VBA.
 Experience with cloud platforms, containerisation, APIs, BI tools, or third‑party integrations.
A relevant degree or equivalent practical experience.

header.name: Myat Pwint Phyu

experience.company: Monash University (Industry collaboration with Energy Safe Victoria)
bullets: Built large-scale compliance monitoring pipeline detecting unsafe electrical products, improving regulatory detection efficiency by 70% across online marketplaces.
Automated Amazon and eBay data collection pipelines, increasing marketplace product coverage by 85% and reducing manual monitoring workload.
Developed AI-based compliance matching system using information retrieval and vector search, reducing manual audit effort by 60%.
Reduced recurring manufacturing defects by 35% through data-driven root cause analysis, quality reporting, and corrective action implementation.
Conducted 40+ quality inspections and audits, ensuring 98% ISO compliance across production and supplier quality processes.
Tracked production quality data using SQL queries and manufacturing tracking systems, improving defect detection and reporting efficiency by 30%.
Optimized production schedules using SAP demand forecasts and Excel automation, improving on-time delivery performance by 20%.
Maintained 95%+ production output targets by monitoring manufacturing KPIs and resolving operational bottlenecks across multiple assembly lines.
Automated production planning reports using Excel, SQL queries, and data connections, reducing manual planning time by 40%.

projects.name: Healthcare Data Management System (Database Project)

bullets: Designed relational healthcare database using SQL and MySQL, improving data integrity and consistency by 40%.
Implemented role-based access control using SQL and database security, reducing unauthorized data access by 35%.

bullets: Developed EEG depression classifier using Python, achieving 98% accuracy.
Engineered EEG alpha/gamma features using MNE and SciPy, improving model performance by 30%.

projects.name: Smart Home Security System with Face Recognition (Academic Project)

bullets: Developed facial recognition security system using OpenCV and Python, improving access authentication accuracy by 92%.
Implemented real-time face detection using OpenCV and Python, reducing unauthorized access by 40%.
